ChIJTTzjGuaNGGARRdGc3fe9gnE.name: カフェ ブラームス cafe BRAHMS

reviews.text: One of the best financiers i have ever eaten . Crispy outside and soft inside with a unique taste. Coffee was also good and well balanced.

I stopped by while wandering around Harajuku because the store had a charming atmosphere and I wanted to support a newly opened business.

I ordered the Neapolitan pasta set with drink, soup, salad, and dessert for 2500¥ (~$16 usd). The pasta was delicious, very chewy and flavorful, but the salad was a bit sad... I forgot to take a picture of my salad but it was about 6 pieces of lettuce maybe and some shredded carrot and beetroot(?). The onion soup was good. I received panna cotta as the dessert. It was tasty, but I thought the portion was a bit small. I also got an oat milk latte, but it was my first time ever having a latte, so I have no valid opinion. It certainly looked beautiful.

The atmosphere truly is lovely and the staff are really nice with good service. It was a relaxing evening dining experience with good ambience. I would want go here again next time I'm in Harajuku just for the pasta.
Nice place to relax. Food and drinks are fine, just wish they listed everything on the menu. (I see people ordering things that aren't on there, like specialty dessert drinks).
Small dedicated lovely temple! Internet said it is famous for praying for love. Very closed to Roppongi station, 3 mins walk! It is up 1 stair. Be aware not to overwalk.
Small shrine in the middle of the cityscape for blessings for relationships
small location and entrance existing quietly, please do not forget four claps

Awesome experience! The intro at the beginning was very useful, it really taught me a lot! Disclaimer, it's entirely in Japanese - so I recommend attending it only if you have a high proficiency. As for the performances, truly outstanding! For that part, subs in ENG and JP are available for free, included in the price of the ticket. It was my first live Nō experience and I believe that anyone who's ever studied it / read about it has to give it a try, seeing it live is an entirely different thing.
Great experience worth to visit. You don’t really need sit next to the stage to enjoy it. Everything is close. English subs included

One of the oldest forms of Japanese traditional performance. If you don’t mind the whole thing is all in Japanese and the pace of the performance is very slow which can probably make some audiences sleepy, you can admire the graciousness and the beauty of the Japanese culture through this show. The whole theater and stage facilities are also fantastic and very well maintained. Make sure to check the program schedule on their website in advance because the show doesn’t run every day and the showtime varies.

ChIJuS2Qp5aNGGARQLYCfvMbZW0.name: GOOD MORNING CAFE NOWADAYS
ChIJuS2Qp5aNGGARQLYCfvMbZW0.address: Japan, 〒160-0015 Tokyo, Shinjuku City, Daikyōchō, 31−４ Brillia ist 千駄ヶ谷 1F

reviews.text:
Western style cafe with a Japanese touch for lunch and dinner. The staff was friendly and professional, the food servings were modest but full of flavour.
I enjoyed the salad the most.

Cute open concept cafe where we got a blend of Japanese and western breakfast. Order by QR code limited menu unlimited coffee or tea with meal. Free wifi. Was a great place to plan the day with next stop the gardens next door

Bad morning cafe
Google reviews, out of respect for actual restaurants, this place shouldn't be allowed to use the label. From start to finish, absolutely terrible.

We walked into an empty cafe and were met with a silent stare from a staffer who didn't even acknowledge us. We seated ourselves and were eventually met by a waiter we'll call Superman, who was oozing a terrible attitude. After ordering via their app, Superman returned with "over easy" eggs that were fried into dark-brown cinder blocks. When I asked for a redo, he argued that "that's how we cook them" and shot me a look like he wanted to start a fight. He then gossiped with the server, Wonderwoman, who joined in by shooting us disapproving looks.

The second egg was barely better, the bacon wasn't crispy, and the sausages were shriveled with "old-age" wrinkles. Then the manager, Napoleon, arrived to greet a VIP at the table next to us. Despite empty tables everywhere, he squeezed between us, his rear partially hanging over our table and cutlery. While the VIPs got foam art lattes, my wife’s identical order was served plain.

As we finished our tasteless meal, Napoleon returned with a coffee carafe. I predicted to my wife we’d get no refill, and I was right. Napoleon wedged his frame between the tables again to serve the VIPs, ignoring our empty cups. I honestly wondered if he had an inflatable device in those pants or was just naturally blessed with huge glutes. He finally unwedged himself and left without a word. I kept waiting for him to turn back to us and yell, "NO COFFEE FOR YOU!"

At the register, Napoleon took our money and offered a backhanded apology: "I'm sorry you don't like the way we cook our eggs." It was likely the first time he’s had to speak to a customer since earning his too-small manager vest and specially reinforced pants. Save your money; this place is a joke.

ChIJKc77NJ2LGGARIaQdb-CsP9U.name: SUZU CAFE Roppongi
ChIJKc77NJ2LGGARIaQdb-CsP9U.address: Japan, 〒106-0032 Tokyo, Minato City, Roppongi, 3-chōme−10−１ 相鉄フレッサイン

reviews.text: The buffet we had on sunday morning was very fulfilling. The scrambled eggs were excellent and the croissants were very light and flaky. The Apple juice tasted like real apples and the berries were so so good in my yoghurt

Came here on our first day in Japan for breakfast. The buffet was laid out nicely and had several choices. Tried a little bit of everything. I would recommend staying away from the scrambled eggs. They were undercooked and cold, but I tried them anyways. Ended up with food poisoning and was very ill. Three days later and I’m finally back on track overall not a bad place and everything else was good.

We got the Matcha pudding, strawberry tart and matcha! The selling point is their matcha milk, it is delicious! Matcha pudding is more bitter and has a strong taste! I think the tart was ok. Our total for 2 matcha milk, 1 tart and 1 pudding was 3,400 yen

ChIJCUP9NSnzGGARTzyUo5fQtfU.name: cafe 明日も発酵ごはん nippon

reviews.text: I had a wonderful dining experience here! The food was beautiful and their lunch set is very yummy. I had the curry set that came with a side salad. They also kindly shared with me their kombucha which was very tasty! I will be returning and I highly recommend. The lady at the store was also very kind

We had an incredibly delicious lunch here at cafe nippon. The curry and the salad set lunch tasted fantastic and were highly creative, wonderfully healthy and beautifully presented. We love fermented foods and it was so interesting to see all the different types of food that can be fermented and taste amazing. Everyone at the cafe was also so friendly and welcoming, taking time to explain the ingredients which we appreciate so much : ) cannot reccommend highly enough!

So happy we stumbled upon this hidden gem. We were warmly greeted with a cup of tea, which set the tone right away. Four of us ordered the course meal, along with the sake sampler and a few additional drinks, everything was absolutely delicious. What really stood out was how thoughtful and healthy the entire meal felt, even down to dessert. A lovely touch was the complimentary tea that kept flowing while we relaxed and enjoyed our time. Such a special, calming experience.

In short, really enjoyed this place. Clean and airy Japanese-influenced design, good quality food and ingredients with fantastic fresh fruits, while pricing/portions were reasonable by Tokyo standards. Being a Brit (albeit a long-time Japan resident), I still find the fruit/cream sandwich concept a little hard to swallow - but these were delicious.

Access by public transport is a little tricky as it’s not near a station (requiring a taxi or bus), but by car it’s straightforward with free parking. There was a queue to get in even at 8am on a Sunday but it moved quickly once it opened.

Would it be worth a journey from central Tokyo as a tourist? I would say yes, just about, as it provides a good Japan experience (high-quality fruit sandwiches! Huge parfaits!) in a different part of the city. The current weak yen also makes the prices good value.

Kajitsuya Cafe truly shines with its specialty fruit sandwiches and parfaits, each bursting with fresh, local and seasonal flavors. This café, known for its clean and spacious setting, offers both comfortable indoor and charming outdoor seating, perfect for any weather. Their menu delights not only with these fruity favorites but also with scrumptious French toast and fresh salads.

For beverages, you're well taken care of with a variety of tea options, robust coffee, and crystal-clear citrus fresh water. Since Kajitsuya is quite the popular spot, arriving early is a smart move to snag a good seat and take your time enjoying their standout offerings. Whether you're in for a quick coffee or a leisurely brunch, Kajitsuya Cafe provides a delightful culinary escape with every visit.

A great place to visit for your morning cafe. You can get a full breakfast (bread, omelets and salad) for less than 10 dollars (1200 yen). The food there was amazing, as well as the service considering how busy they usually are.

Great little cafe bakery. Enjoyed a nice Thai style lunch here. Decor is beautiful, food is tasty and service is friendly. Would recommend and come back. Baked sweets look good, but didn’t try them this time.

Cards & digital payments available.

Bike parking is available, so could be a nice stop when cycling by the Tamagawa.
A nice cafe not so far from Tamagawa. It’s good stop when you’re cycling in Tamagawa. The food is tasty and staff is friendly. I would definitely visit again.
Had a 2nd breakfast after riding along the river. Coffee, bread and sallad, delicious.

First time visiting a racecourse in Japan! The racecourse is amazingly big with food stalls selling. I'm impressed the atmosphere there and the staff were so nice to teach me how to  buy one round before the race started! Although I couldn't win a game, I enjoyed a lot there with the sincere explanation from the staff and the atmosphere (yeah, everyone so concentrated 😂). The racecourse is very very big and it takes 20 minutes walk to the museum 🐎

A truly spectacular racecourse to visit. The museum is amazing with all the past champions on show,the turfy shops , food and drink plus the ambience was amazing, even on a listed race day. The entrance fee of 200Yen is quite remarkable compared to Australia and casual attire makes it a perfect day out for all.

A great place to experience horse racing.  You will see families with babies and children coming here as weekend excursion/outing. This place is family friendly, you will be surprised! Lotsnof food outlets here too. Spacious, great for picnic too!

reviews.text:
The first thing you notice here is that the staff are extremely friendly and very welcoming.

The environment and designs are very nice and aquatic, which suits the shop name.

The shisha recommendation by the staff was really good.

Will definitely come back on my next trips !

I came here alone after midnight during my vacation to Tokyo. The vibe was absolutely wonderful. Loved the dim, ambient lighting, chill music, and the blue fish tank. This place was perfect to relax alone for a few hours and enjoy shisha and drinks. I will definitely return next time I visit Japan.

Great Shisha Bar in Akasaka Mitsuke. The ambiente is super relaxed, the big aquarium is awesome! Shisha was good, as well as the drinks we had. My recommendation: try the unsweetened ice tea if you want to have something without alcohol. We will for sure come back next time were here! :)

ChIJOZ_4EwCNGGAR7BxJTkOswEY.name: Muffin&Bowls cafe CUPS 表参道店｜かき氷&マフィン
ChIJOZ_4EwCNGGAR7BxJTkOswEY.address: Japan, 〒150-0001 Tokyo, Shibuya, Jingūmae, 3-chōme−5−１ 神宮前３５１ビル 1F

reviews.text: The strawberry condensed milk smoothie was delicious! The staff were also very friendly and helpful! Excellent service.

A popular shop from Machida has opened its second location in Omotesando! 🥳
The spacious interior allows strollers to enter easily!
There were a few steps at the entrance, but my daughter was asleep so they helped us out. 🥹💦

When it was brought out, it was so cute, I was thrilled! 🫶🏻
And the portion size was huge!!

The regular and mini sizes were only 200 yen apart, so I chose the regular size! 🤭❣️

Luckily, they had white strawberries in stock! 🍓🤍

The pie was homemade!! Super crispy! ♡♡
And there was even pie inside the shaved ice! Surprisingly, it wasn't soggy and remained crispy. 🤤
There were also raisins inside, which added a nice texture. 😋

Overall, it was topped with a diplomate cream, a sauce that tasted like custard cream, so it really felt like I was eating a Napoleon pie!!

The ice was so fluffy, and even with this large portion, I was able to enjoy it deliciously until the very last bite! 🤗

The thoughtful gesture of offering warm hojicha tea to accompany the shaved ice was wonderful! 😍✨

There are four types of cream soda.

This time I chose the blue cream soda.

The color is so cute! 🩵

• Napoleon Pie Regular Size (¥2860)
• Blue Cream Soda (¥880)

I went to @cups_omotesando, a stylish cafe where you can enjoy shaved ice and freshly baked muffins!

Here's what I ordered:
* Shaved Ice - Napoleon Pie (Regular) ¥2,860
* Blue Cream Soda ¥880

You can choose from four types of shaved ice (Napoleon Pie / Sakura Mont Blanc and Almond Tofu / Sakura Chocolate Mint / Strawberry S'mores and Milk Tea)!
This time I chose Napoleon Pie!
Here's what it consisted of:
* Powdered sugar
* Fresh strawberries
* Diplomat cream
* Caramelized crust
* Roasted almonds
* Strawberry syrup
* Anglaise sauce
* Three kinds of raisins
* Crispy crust
* Sliced ​​strawberries
* Homemade condensed milk

The regular size is huge! Eating it alone leaves you feeling as satisfied as if you'd eaten a whole cake! The balance of sweetness and tartness was exquisite, with fresh strawberries (white strawberries are also available this time of year!), crispy pie crust, cream, strawberry syrup, and condensed milk!

The shaved ice was filled with plenty of raisins and pie crust, allowing you to enjoy the changing textures and flavors!

The ice was incredibly fluffy and melted instantly in your mouth!

It had a gentle and light texture!

There are five types of cream soda!

This time I chose the blue cream soda!

The beautiful blue color makes it perfect for photos!

It tasted like cider!

The vanilla ice cream on top was large, smooth, and absolutely delicious!

I was able to relax and enjoy a special space in a stylish and calming atmosphere!

It's perfect for the upcoming hot season!

I highly recommend you all go and try it! ✨

The Meiji Jingu Baseball Stadium is very old. It has a lot of character. The food here is amazing. So many choices and very affordable compare to a US sporting event. For example, I was a 49ers game this year and beers were $19.50 compared to $6 beers here. We had tickets along the first base line on row 22. They were only around $60 USD for a rivalry baseball game, Tokyo Swallows vs Tokyo Giants. We had a lot of popcorn, hot dogs and noodles. They were only around $7-10 USD. The cherry on top was that they showed us on the big jumbotron.

There is a smoking area by Gate 7.

The stadium itself is well kept and easy to navigate. There’s food and souvenir options on the perimeter of the stadium, as well as some food options inside. Brought a backpack to store my stuff and didn’t have any issues. It was a great experience!! Don’t forget your umbrella!! We are the Swallows!!

I'd say this stadium has a very different feel with Tokyo Dome. Watching a baseball game here was fun and a new experience for me since I'm usually at the Tokyo Dome, which is a closed stadium.

Anyway, the vibe sure is different.
I'm just thankful that the weather on the day we watched the game was fine. I heard games can be cancelled when the rain gets harsh.

Will definitely come back 🤙🏽🤙🏽

Just had my second successful hip replacement operation at Meirikal Tokyo Yamato Hospital in two years. The professionalism, level and quality of care was first rate, again. They were friendly and very patient with me, making communication in English very easy. I couldn’t be more pleased with the outcome.

I recently underwent kidney stone surgery at this hospital, and I couldn't be more impressed with the care I received. From the knowledgeable and compassionate medical staff to the state-of-the-art facilities, every aspect of my experience exceeded my expectations. The procedure went smoothly, and I felt well-cared for throughout my stay. I highly recommend this for anyone in need of kidney stone treatment.

Visited for a long-term urinary problem that I had struggled to get other health practicioners to take seriously. My Japanese is limited, so I was somewhat anxious at first, but this was by far the easiest and most reassuring hospital visit I have ever had.

On arriving at reception with a referral from my primary practicioner, the staff immediately flagged down a fluent English-speaking nurse who assisted me with the initial paperwork and ensuring I understood where to go and what was happening. My doctor was also excellent both in her English abilities and her professional manner, successfully diagnosing my problem within minutes of speaking with me and quickly confirming it with minimal testing.

All necessary examinations and procedures were carried out in the same visit, and at each stage all staff made my comfort and understanding their utmost priority. My visit finished with a follow-up appointment being arranged to ensure that my recovery was going smoothly. For any English-speaking visitors or residents in the Tokyo area seeking a friendly and capable urology hospital, I can confidently recommend this one as top-of-the-line.

There are only five counter seats, so it's recommended for small groups and with limited luggage. More customers opt for takeout than dine-in.

Enjoy scones, egg tarts, and savory tarts with hot coffee or tea.

This shop just opened in October 2025.
When I visited at 3:00 PM, there were two customers eating in and one customer ordering takeout.
There was only one pie left, made with Kagoshima black pork, but the other pie was still available.

When I dine in, I had the Matcha Cream Puff.
It was sprinkled with matcha powder on top, wrapped in paper and eaten like a hamburger.
The cream inside had a faint matcha flavor, but was not too sweet and delicious.

I think the tea was Assam? The plump mug was cute.

I also ordered the Egg Tart to go, which I had been interested in after reading the reviews.
This one was filled with soft custard and had a gentle sweetness.
The pie crust was moist and made a perfect gift.

Thank you for the meal!

The egg tarts are a must-buy! The pies and cream puffs are delicious, too. However, if I were to buy just one item as a souvenir, I would definitely choose the egg tarts! This shop looks set to become a new staple in the area! I wholeheartedly recommend it!

Honestly, I want to gatekeep this place but I've been a few times and the staff has always been so sweet, I feel like I need to leave a review :) Very cozy, very kind workers. The coffee is so good and rich! I always get the standard plate because the yogurt and granola are so good. Definitely worth coming here for a meal and to sit and relax!
To get there, you go in through the front lobby of the apartment building. Go to the elevators and up to the second floor. It'll be right there when you get out of the elevator :)

How have I lived in Itabashi for 4 months and just discovered this place? You feel like you are going to visit a friend in their apartment. It’s kinda like going to one of the hidden speak easy bars like JANAI (which you should try as well!). Fantastic vibe, good eclectic music playing from good quality speakers. Nice open space with tons of natural light coming through a wall of glass looking out on the neighborhood. I had a breakfast set with a hot latte and every ingredient was quality and perfectly prepared. So delicious! I will definitely be back…and back again!

We've been staying in Ikebukuro during our trip, and I decided to check out this place as the aesthetic looked good from pictures. So happy I did, as the latte I got was delicious!

The place can be a little tricky to locate as it is on the second floor of a residential-looking building, and signage is easy to miss. Great spot, so don't miss out!

Since moving to Tokyo earlier this year, I’ve made Tokyo Union Church my spiritual home. Initially, I was drawn in by its photos on Google Maps and its central location. But what truly convinced me to stay was the welcoming and genuinely open atmosphere.

Every service leaves me uplifted and inspired. The messages are always relevant, thoughtfully delivered, and offer fresh, practical insights that stay with me. I especially value that the church approaches the Bible with a balanced and thoughtful perspective—never extreme or dogmatic, but always inclusive and respectful of different viewpoints.

One unexpected delight has been the choir, whose level of talent and coordination genuinely astonished me; it feels almost like attending a professional concert each week.

It’s my kind of church.
A very warm and welcoming Church community which I am grateful to attend. A great place to get to know fellow brothers and sisters in Christ. Truly have nothing negative to say here 👏
I am glad that our family did attend the special Christmas service on Christmas Day!

ChIJ5W88Bb6rNTERS1bmOSwQuwQ.name: Vietnam Tuong Theatre
ChIJ5W88Bb6rNTERS1bmOSwQuwQ.address: 51A P. Đường Thành, Cửa Đông, Hoàn Kiếm, Hà Nội, Vietnam

reviews.text: An notable building strolling from Old Quarter to Train Street. The road traffic is crazy.

The Vietnam Tuong Theatre also known as the Hanoi Opera House has nothing to envy from  such theatres around the world. it has beautiful design, great decor and a true class act. I really enjoyed being there.

Sometimes going down the wrong street is just the beginning of something great. Jumped into a coffee shop for some respite from the heat and a Saigon Coffee. Watched the Theater from across the road and visited after a little second hand cigarette smoke. A great little theater and so a tip, you can pay outside for what you can afford. The Theater has some great displayed costumes, weapons and masks. I watched the Lune production Lang Toi (My Village) honestly the best show of seen this side of a Movie, Tv, or Theater Screen. So thats why the 5. the Timing show and location couldn't have been better.

ChIJVW7dj9GrNTERfnxk5lvzCB0.address: Tầng 6, Chung Cư Mipec Riverside, P. Long Biên 2, Ngọc Lâm, Bồ Đề, Hà Nội 100000, Vietnam

reviews.text: This is the best cinema in Long Bien. The popcorn is actually warm and fresh. The picture and sound is very good. Most importantly it's very clean and I haven't seen any insects here.

The best cinema in town!!! Their service is excellent, the design of each room is clever with the exit on both sides so you don't have to pass the entire line to get out if your seat is at the end of the row. They do have vending machine which I think is very convenient and smart to purchase tickets. Absolutely love this cinema!!!
Good place to watch movies but need more cleaning inside of the cinema mall hall

ChIJvQcdAeuOGGAR7CHjoH_Pdnw.name: Keisuke Gyoza Bar
ChIJvQcdAeuOGGAR7CHjoH_Pdnw.address: Japan, 〒111-0032 Tokyo, Taito City, Asakusa, 4-chōme−18−７ メゾンフジ

reviews.text: I found Keiskue Gyoza Bar on Google and there are no words to describe how absolutely wonderful the experience was. I went with my younger brother, met some absolutely wonderful people, and had one of the best experiences I've had in Japan. The gyoza was, to quote my brother, an 11 out of 10. The atmosphere was warm and welcoming. It truly felt like we were in the kitchen with Keiskue. When I heard he brewed his own sake, I had to order a bottle to share with new friends. It was some of the best sake I've ever had. It was light, crisp, dry and dangerous. You don't taste the alcohol. Keiskue-sama, if you're reading this, thank you for one of the best experiences I've ever had.

A super cute and tiny spot, it feels more like stepping into someone’s home than a restaurant. The setup is really unique, you pick your own drink from a small bar hanging on the wall. The gyozas might not be the best you’ve ever had, but the overall experience is so charming that it totally makes up for it. I’d definitely come back just for that!

I would thoroughly recommend this to anyone.

Keisuke provided us with a wonderful and entertaining experience. We learnt to fold our Gyoza - we did terribly I might add - but being engaged in the evening was a joy.

The restaurant has about 6-8 seats and I ordered the sparkling sake which was delicious. For gyoza, we had mushroom and ginger, potato and cheese (so yum), vegetable seasoned with leek and garlic, and dipped these in a homemade miso dipping sauce.

Such a fun, delicious and entertaining night

ChIJDTq-ORWPGGARfLngswbJIWU.name: Awakening Cafe
ChIJDTq-ORWPGGARfLngswbJIWU.address: Japan, 〒110-0014 Tokyo, Taito City, Kitaueno, 2-chōme−23−９ イチロービル 4階, Ichiro Building 4th floor

reviews.text: Great little hole in the wall cafe. Staff was super nice and spoke excellent english. The prices were very good and I can't recommend the Carbonara enough it was delicious. I also had the Dessert waffles and the S'more hot chocolate the day before and they were excellent as well. The owner is super welcoming and wholesome.

highly recommended this cafe!! i don’t drink coffee often but this place was worth the stop! the service is amazing & people inside were very friendly/welcoming as well. cozy atmosphere to read or do work.

The atmosphere is okay.
They would asked to pay your order after they take your order.
Our drinks came 30 minutes after, then the food arrived almost an hour after. The last order came more than an hour after all the foods have been served.
If you have nothing to go to after, this is the place for you.
But if you have things to do expecting your drinks & food within 30 minutes, be ready to wait & wait.
Wonderful place for Theatre performance.
nice show
It says they are too tired to open. I hope they get better soon...

ChIJ8_V1CguPGGARYav_lMkiQGU.name: SANSOU CAFE
ChIJ8_V1CguPGGARYav_lMkiQGU.address: Japan, 〒111-0032 Tokyo, Taito City, Asakusa, 3-chōme−15−２ タンジェリン浅草

reviews.text: A hidden gem for healthy eating in Asakusa, vegan-friendly. I had shio-koji salmon and genmai onigiri, but the chicken tofu burger and vegan curry looked delicious too! The ambience was calming, the food nurturing, and the staff were very friendly and attentive. Will definitely come back with my vegetarian husband!

I ordered the Yakuzen curry with a chicken topping, and it was absolutely delicious! honestly, I thought the portion looked small at first, but I was wrong. It was actually very filling!
They also have a huge selection of drinks and cafe items. Asakusa is always so crowded, but this place was calm and relaxing—a true oasis. I definitely want to come back next time I’m in Asakusa!
I highly recommend this place for vegans and those on a gluten-free diet.

We stopped by this cafe while we were waiting for a dinner reservation and it’s a very welcoming and warm cafe with great staff and service. We had the vegan curry and appetizer dish with home cream cheese (bomb!). Also their glass of red wine was delicious. Stop by if you are in the area!

## 縁結びの神様 出雲大社

![Image 1: 出雲大社東京分祠](https://izumotaisya-tokyobunshi.com/images/home1.png)

ここ六本木の出雲大社東京分祠は出雲大社の御祭神大国主大神の御分霊を奉斎する都内唯一の分祠であり、 東部の要として、第八十代国造、出雲大社教初代管長の千家尊福公によって明治の初期に創設されました。

出雲大社の御祭神、大国主大神（おおくにぬしのおおかみ）は縁結びの神として、広く知られておりますが、 縁結びとは単に男女の縁を結ぶということだけではありません。私どもが立派に成長しますように、 社会が明るく楽しいものであるようにとお互いの幸福のためのすばらしい縁がむすばれるということなのです。

![Image 2: 祭儀の様子](https://izumotaisya-tokyobunshi.com/images/home2.png)

ダイコクさま、福の神として親しまれている大国主大神さまが、多くの方々から広く深い信仰を受けておられるのも この「むすび」という愛情を私どもに限りなくそそいでくださるからです。

当分祠では皆様方が、ダイコクさまの幸せのご縁をいただかれて、 明るく安らかに過ごすことが出来ますよう、日々お祈りいたしております。

## ご案内

![Image 3: 御祈願](https://izumotaisya-tokyobunshi.com/images/prayer.png)

### 御祈願

家内安全、商売繁盛、縁結、交通安全、初宮参り、七五三、厄除など、様々な御祈願を承っております。午前10時より午後3時まで、1時間毎に随時行っております。

[詳しく見る](https://izumotaisya-tokyobunshi.com/prayer.html)

![Image 4: 神前結婚式](https://izumotaisya-tokyobunshi.com/images/wedding_about2.jpg)

### 神前結婚式

明治31年より神前結婚式を執り行い、東京における神前結婚式の端緒を開きました。出雲式の祭儀を以って皆様の御婚儀を御奉仕申上げます。

[詳しく見る](https://izumotaisya-tokyobunshi.com/wedding.html)

![Image 5: 御札・御守](https://izumotaisya-tokyobunshi.com/images/shichigosan1.png)

### 御札・御守

御玉串、御木札、関札、釜社札、縁結守、縁むすびの糸、壮気健全守、厄除守、開運守など、各種御札・御守をお授けしております。

[詳しく見る](https://izumotaisya-tokyobunshi.com/omamori.html)

2026年4月2日

いつもSOL’S COFFEEの定期便をご利用いただき、誠にありがとうございます。 このたび、原材料費や輸送コストの高騰、 ならびに今後も安定した品質でコーヒーをお届けし続けるため、 2026年4月1日より、定期便の内容をリニューアルさせていただくことになりました。 それに伴い、価格・ラインナップ・発送頻度を下記の通り変更いたします。 ⬛︎ プラン詳細 【 毎月届く、いまのSOL'S COFFEE 】 飲む量やライフスタイルに合わせて、 ３種類のコースから定期便がお選びいただけます。  ①100g×2種(定番2種) 毎月のコーヒーに、ちょうどいいセット SOL'S COFFEEの定番ラインナップから、 バランスよく楽しめる2種類をセレクト。 「まずSOL'S COFFEEの豆を試したい」 「毎日飲むコーヒーを安定して楽しみたい」 そんな方におすすめのベーシックな内容です。  ②50g×3種(限定１種＋定番2種) 少しずつ、いろんな味を楽しみたい方へ 定番2種類に、その月におすすめの限定豆を1種類同封。 「新しい豆も気になるけど、飲みきれるか不安」 「その日の気分で豆を変えたい」...

2026年2月12日

いつもSOL’S COFFEEをご利用いただき、ありがとうございます。 このたび、オンラインストアでの豆の販売量が変更となりました。  ------------- 【変更前】100g 【変更後】150g / 250g / 500g 3種類からお選びいただけるようになりました！ -------------  今回の変更は、 ご自宅でのコーヒーの楽しみ方やペースに合わせて、 選んでいただける形にしたいという思いからです。 それぞれの販売量によって、 250gは250円お得,500gは750円お得 となる、値段設定となっております。  焙煎したてのコーヒーを、 その時の暮らしや気分に合わせて、 気持ちよく楽しんでいただけたら嬉しいです。  これからも、 毎日のコーヒーが心地よくなるような一杯をお届けしていきます。 引き続き、SOL’S COFFEEをどうぞよろしくお願いいたします。

年末年始の営業についてのお知らせ🎍 いつもSOL’S COFFEEをご利用いただき誠にありがとうございます。 年末年始の各店舗・オンラインストアの営業スケジュールは下記の通りとなります。 ⸻ 【 ROASTERY 】 12/29・12/30 休業 12/31 9:00-14:00（※豆販売・ドリンクのみ） 1/1・1/2 休業 1/3〜 通常営業 ⸻ 【 SOLAMACHI 】 通常営業 ⸻ 【 APARTMENT 】 12/28(日) 9:00-17:00 12/29(月)〜1/5(月) 休業 1/6(火)〜 通常営業 ⸻ 【 ONLINE STORE 】 年内最終発送：12/29...

2025年4月1日

SOL’S COFFEEをいつもご利用いただき、誠にありがとうございます。この度、2025年4月より、原材料の価格高騰や品質向上のためのコスト増加により、メニューの価格を変更させていただくことになりました。より良い品質とサービスの提供を継続するために、何卒ご理解賜りますようお願い申し上げます。 これからもお客様に美味しいコーヒーをお届けできるよう最大限努力してまいります。 今後ともSOL’S COFFEEをよろしくお願いいたします。  ★ 豆販売の容量が、 150g→100gに変更となっております。 いつもご利用してくださる方にはご不便をおかけし致しますが、ご理解いただけますと幸いです

2024年6月28日

創業から15周年を迎えたSOL’S COFFEEが、2013年から11年間に渡って拠点を構えてきた蔵前。その蔵前一号店としてオープンしたSOL’S COFFEE STANDが、2024年5月17日をもって閉店を迎えました。一つの節目となるこのタイミングで、蔵前を代表するホステルの一つでもあるNui.を運営するBackpackers' Japan取締役の石崎さんとSOL’S COFFEE代表アライリエコの対談インタビューが実現。同じく根を下ろす蔵前という土地を出発点に、人が集まる場や魅力的なまちの形成、これからの蔵前について伺いました。アライリエコ2009年にSOL’S COFFEEを立ち上げ、キッチンカーでの移動販売と自家焙煎コーヒー豆の通信販売を開始。その後、蔵前にてコーヒースタンドSOL’S COFFEEをオープン。現在では都内に5店舗、福井・若狭町に1店舗を構える。2022年、家業でもある「荒井鉄工所」の意志を継ぎ、日本製の珈琲器具メーカープロジェクト quissaco（キッサコ）を始動。エスプレッソメーカーや焙煎機の開発を手掛ける。石崎嵩人2010年に友人３人とともに株式会社Backpackers' Japanを創業。Nui. Title: 浅草カフェ・SANSOU CAFE- サンソウカフェ-  酵素玄米ごはんとグルテンフリースイーツカフェ・ヴィーガンメニュー・夜カフェ

URL Source: https://sansoucafe.com/

Markdown Content:
![Image 1](https://sansoucafe.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/p1.png)

![Image 2](https://sansoucafe.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/p3-1.jpg)

![Image 3](https://sansoucafe.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/p2-1.jpg)

浅草でゆっくり過ごせるカフェをお探しの方へ。

SANSOU CAFEでは、季節の食材と厳選した調味料を使い、身体にやさしい「基本食」を丁寧に手作りしています。

添加物を控え、消化に良い酵素玄米を中心に、心と身体が自然と整う食事を気軽に楽しめるカフェです。

浅草寺や雷門からも歩いてアクセスでき、観光の合間の休憩や、地元の方の日常使いにもご利用いただいています。

グルテンフリースイーツ・ヴィーガンメニューございます。

[【５月営業スケジュール】 （SANSOU CAFE 営業日） 水木：11:00-16:00 金土：](https://www.instagram.com/p/DXehNMgFPoP/)

[今月の季節のケーキは抹茶のガトーショコラです。 濃厚な抹茶の風味のケーキですので 是非お召し上がり](https://www.instagram.com/p/DXbD-UKjj0O/)

[- sansou cafe のさつまいもケーキは 蒸籠で蒸したさつまいもたっぷりの、健康的なグルテン](https://www.instagram.com/p/DXN5pIAD_RA/)

[薄着の季節になってきましたね！ sansou cafeガパオライスとハンバーグは 鶏胸肉とお豆腐で作](https://www.instagram.com/p/DXLRRn4j6_V/)

[SANSOU CAFEはお子様連れ大歓迎です![Image 4: 🕊️](https://s.w.org/images/core/emoji/17.0.2/svg/1f54a.svg) ゆったり過ごせる空間で、ほっとひと息つきませんか？](https://www.instagram.com/p/DXI8u9vFNux/)

[このたび、SANSOU CAFEが日本洋菓子協会連合会機関誌「ガトー」に掲載されました。 このような](https://www.instagram.com/p/DW7zYiXD7yi/)

[sansou cafeは 都バス西浅草3丁目停留所から すぐの場所にあります❀ 近くは一葉桜(花と葉](https://www.instagram.com/p/DW2oTX6j1mL/)

[SANSOU CAFEのお料理はすべてグルテンフリーです。 ランチは16時までお召し上がりいただけま](https://www.instagram.com/p/DWp3l3Lj-nR/)

[Instagram でフォロー](https://www.instagram.com/sansoucafeasakusa/)

## ABOUT US

食を通して、笑顔がめぐる場所へ

SANSOU CAFEは「心からくつろげる時間」を大切に、季節の食材とやさしい調理で身体に負担の少ない料理を提供します。栄養豊富な酵素玄米を中心に、地元の方から観光の方まで気軽に楽しめる場所を目指しています。

[詳しく見る](https://sansoucafe.com/concept/)

![Image 5](https://sansoucafe.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/e3c0a4df53a2d4dd57ee1a5f933dd271.png)

## MENU

食をととのえるということ

SANSOU CAFEの料理は、身体が必要としている栄養をしっかり取り入れながら、

心まで満たされるように丁寧にお作りしています。

季節の食材を生かし、消化を助けるスパイスや厳選した調味料を使い、

添加物やトランス脂肪酸は極力使用していません。

また、ヴィーガンやグルテンフリーにも対応しております。

[詳しく見る](https://sansoucafe.com/menu/)

![Image 6](https://sansoucafe.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/f1933603f4c63e9704a20ac60914d4d2.png)
